grades.label: Market Position
grades.evidence: Dominant in warehouse-club retail; ~90 million paying members. The 10-K describes a price-leadership flywheel — scale buying → lower prices → more members → more scale.

grades.label: Moat Type & Strength
grades.evidence: Membership renewal rate >90%. Treasure-hunt SKU rotation builds visit habit. Combined scale economies and trust brand. The flywheel is documented across multiple Letters.

grades.label: Market Quality
grades.evidence: Grocery and general-merchandise retail; modest secular growth. Less optionality than tech but more durable in any macro regime.

grades.label: Leadership Quality
grades.evidence: Craig Jelinek (CEO from 2012) maintains the Sinegal-era price discipline. Compensation modest by S&P 500 standards. Capital returned via special dividends rather than buybacks at peak multiples.

pullquote: "The engine saw a strong position, wide moat, stable market, and disciplined leadership — the textbook compounder profile. It assigned 46% probability to 'good' and 25% to 'great'. The realised return through 2026 sits near the high end of the 'good' bucket."
pullquote_attr: From the demo notebook · 2017 vintage holdout
